summary:
Major strategic discussion about the future of AnswerAI's technology stack. Brad revealed that Flowwise has Enterprise-licensed components baked into core functionality (workspaces, authentication), creating legal risk. Max built the workplaces feature on Enterprise-licensed code despite being told not to. They need to migrate off Flowwise urgently. Brad proposed replacing the chat interface with open-source alternatives like AnythingLLM (MIT license) or LibreChat (MIT license), while keeping Data Engine for document stores and analysis.

Adam discussed the upcoming Diligent categorization project - building an in-Sanity plugin for AI-powered content tagging across topics, roles, and companies. They debated whether to use Data Engine or hit the Grok API directly for the initial implementation. Brad recommended hitting Grok directly for next week's deadline and pitching Data Engine as an upsell. The categorization uses a voting mechanism with multiple cheap model passes for accuracy. They also discussed the broader vision of Data Engine as a data lake for content gap analysis, reporting, and cross-source analytics.

Additional topics included graph databases (Neo4j) for relationship mapping and content conflict detection, the potential for Diligent to explore CMS alternatives to Contentful (possibly Sanity or Salesforce Knowledge Base), and the concept of using Data Engine's tagging and analysis capabilities as a sellable feature for clients like IAS and Diligent.
keyDiscussionPoints: Flowwise Enterprise license violation - must migrate off urgently
Open-source chat replacements: AnythingLLM and LibreChat (both MIT)
Diligent content categorization project - topics, roles, companies tagging
Data Engine vs direct API for categorization implementation
Graph databases (Neo4j) for content relationship mapping and duplicate detection
Diligent evaluating CMS alternatives to Contentful (Sanity, Salesforce KB)
Alpha Agent documentation with auto-generated screenshots via Playwright
decisions: Must sunset Flowwise due to Enterprise license risk
For next week's Diligent categorization: hit Grok API directly, not build on Flowwise
No more infrastructure should be built on Flowwise chat flows
Need gap analysis of current customer use cases before full migration
Alpha Agent cloud deployment planned for shared access

actionItems.action: Build Diligent categorization plugin using direct Grok API
Create use case parity analysis for Flowwise migration
Build Sanity data source integration for Data Engine
Finish CSV upload feature for Data Engine demos
Deploy Alpha Agent to cloud instance
Check what IAS users are actually using in Flowwise
Create ticket for Jamie to research Sanity field-level localization plugin

followUp: Data Engine Sanity integration for future use cases
